title: atractocarpus hirtus
intro: Atractocarpus hirtus, also known as the hairy stinkwood, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been found to aid in the treatment of various health issues. The health benefits of Atractocarpus hirtus include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Atractocarpus hirtus can be made into a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties for therapeutic purposes.
